Growth Stats: How Many People Subscribe?Written by Brian DeanLast updated on Mar. 13, 2026Netflix was founded in 1997 as a service that shipped DVDs to customers by mail. In 2007, Netflix launched the product we’re most familiar with today: streaming movies and TV over the Internet.
In 2001, Netflix had fewer than half a million subscribers. Today, it has 325 million paying members, and the company’s market cap is approaching $400 billion.
Read on to find all of the latest Netflix statistics for 2026.
Here’s a breakdown of what we’ll cover:
Netflix Statistics (Top Picks)
How Many Subscribers Does Netflix Have?

How Many People Work at Netflix?
Netflix Statistics (Top Picks)
Netflix currently has 325 million subscribers, up from 167.09 million members in 2019.
Netflix generated $45.2 billion in revenue in 2025.
Around 2 in 5 titles available on Netflix’s US catalog are originals or exclusives.
Around 16,000 employees work at Netflix.
How Many Subscribers Does Netflix Have?
According to company-reported data, Netflix has over 325 million subscribers worldwide.
Since Q4 2020, the number of Netflix paying members has grown by approximately 60%.
We’ve charted the growth of Netflix’s subscriber base since 2001:

Netflix subscribers by region
According to company data, Netflix has 89.63 million paying subscribers based in the US & Canada. Netflix’s user base in the US & Canada accounts for a 29.68% share of global Netflix subscribers.
In fact, since 2018, Netflix has added approximately 25 million new subscribers based in the US and Canada.
Here’s an overview of Netflix subscriber growth in the US and Canada since 2018:

Netflix has 101.3 million paying subscribers based in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. 33.54% of worldwide Netflix paying members are based in the EMEA region.
In Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, Netflix has gained over 63 million new memberships since 2018.

Since 2018, Netflix has more than doubled its user base in Latin America, increasing by over 27 million.

Netflix Content Spending

How Much Original Content Is on Netflix?
Original and Exclusive content accounts for 61.89% of titles in Netflix’s US catalog. That’s an increase from a 20.6% share of original and exclusive content in March 2019.
Since 2022, over 2,000 Netflix Originals have been made available on the platform.

Time Spent Watching Netflix
On average, US adults spend 62.1 minutes a day watching Netflix. That’s up from 53.3 minutes per day in 2019.

How many people work at Netflix?
Netflix has around 16,000 full-time employees worldwide. The vast majority (10,900) of them are based in the US and Canada.
Here’s a detailed breakdown of the number of Netflix employees by region:

The median compensation for software engineers at Netflix in the US totals approximately $370,00.
Source: Netflix, Levels FYI
Conclusion
That’s it for my roundup of Netflix usage and revenue statistics in 2026.
Netflix continues to lead the way among streaming services, with over 325 million subscribers.
Yet, Netflix is positioned in a very competitive environment. Amazon Prime Video has around 200 million subscribers, while Disney+ has over 130 million.Backlinko is owned by Semrush.
